revision.text:
[[en:Gokiso Hachimangū]][[fr:Gokiso Hachiman-gū]][[ja:御器所八幡宮]] {{Expand Japanese|御器所八幡宮}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Gokiso Hachimangū | native_name = 御器所八幡宮 | image = Gokiso-Hachimangu02.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Gokiso Hachimangū | coordinates = {{coord|35.14563889|136.92341111}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Ōjin|ar|الإمبراطور أوجين|arz|الامبراطور اوجين|azb|اوجین (ایمپیراتور)|bn|সম্রাট ওজিন|cs|Ódžin|de|Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|es|Ōjin Tennō|eu|Ōjin enperadorea|fa|امپراتور اوجین|fi|Ōjin|fr|Ōjin|hr|Ōjin|id|Kaisar Ōjin|it|Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|ka|ოძინი|ko|오진 천황|nl|Ojin|pl|Ōjin|pt|Ojin|ru|Император Одзин|sh|Car Ōjin|simple|Emperor Ōjin|sl|Cesar Odžin|sv|Ojin|th|จักรพรรดิโอจิง|tl|Emperador Ōjin|tr|Ōjin (imparator)|uk|Імператор Одзін|uz|Imperator Ōjin|vi|Thiên hoàng Ōjin|yo|Emperor Ōjin|zh_min_nan|Ôzin Thian-hông|zh_yue|應神天皇|zh|应神天皇|qid=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} }} {{nihongo|'''Gokiso Hachiman-gū'''|御器所八幡宮}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Q134917286|ltq=Q134917286|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Q11390939|ltq=Q11390939|qid=Q11390939|1=Hachiman shrine}} * {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} * {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|lt=Kokuhei-sha|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Owari Province|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Owari Province|ja|尾張国の式内社一覧|qid=Q11465292|1=List of Shikinaisha in Owari Province}} ** Series ordinal: 107 ** P155: {{ill|Q135039551|ltq=Q135039551|qid=Q135039551|1=Mitano Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Kaharano Shrine|lt=Kaharano Shrine|qid=Q135039553|1=Kaharano Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q11751|ltq=Q11751|qid=Q11751|1=Nagoya}} * {{ill|Q514506|ltq=Q514506|qid=Q514506|1=Owari Province}} * {{ill|Aichi District|lt=Aichi district|ceb|Aichi-gun|de|Aichi-gun|en|Aichi District|es|Distrito de Aichi (Aichi)|fa|شهرستان آیچی، آیچی|fr|District d'Aichi|hu|Aicsi körzet|id|Distrik Aichi, Aichi|it|Distretto di Aichi|ja|愛知郡 (愛知県)|ko|아이치군|pl|Powiat Aichi|ru|Айти (уезд)|tl|Distrito ng Aichi, Aichi|uk|Повіт Айті|vi|Aichi (huyện)|zh|愛知郡 (愛知縣)|qid=Q552687|1=Aichi District}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|35.14563889|136.92341111}} == image (P18) == * Gokiso-Hachimangu02.jpg == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Kokugakuin University Shrine database|lt=Kokugakuin University Shrine database|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|ltq=Q3029647|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == country (P17) == * Улас|ca|Japó|canews|Categoria:Japó|caquote|Japó|cbk_zam|Japón|cdo|Nĭk-buōng|ceb|Hapon|ce|Ипнойчоь|chr|ᏣᏩᏂᏏ|ch|Chapan|chy|Japan|ckb|ژاپۆن|co|Giappone|crh|Japon|csb|Japòńskô|cs|Japonsko|csnews|Kategorie:Japonsko|csquote|Japonsko|csvoyage|Japonsko|cu|Ꙗпѡнїꙗ|cv|Япони|cy|Japan|dag|Japan|da|Japan|de|Japan|denews|Kategorie:Japan|dequote|Japan|devoyage|Japan|din|Japan|diq|Japonya|dsb|Japańska|dtp|Jepun|dty|जापान|dv|ޖަޕާނު|dz|ཇ་པཱན|ee|Japan|el|Ιαπωνία|elnews|Κατηγορία:Ιαπωνία|elquote|Ιαπωνία|elvoyage|Ιαπωνία|eml|Giapòṅ|en|Japan|ennews|Category:Japan|enquote|Japan|envoyage|Japan|eo|Japanio|eonews|Kategorio:Japanujo|eoquote|Japanio|eovoyage|Japanio|es|Japón|esnews|Categoría:Japón|esquote|Japón|esvoyage|Japón|et|Jaapan|etquote|Jaapan|eu|Japonia|ext|Japón|fa|ژاپن|fanews|رده:ژاپن|faquote|ژاپن|favoyage|ژاپن|ff|Japan|fiu_vro|Jaapan|fi|Japani|finews|Luokka:Japani|fivoyage|Japani|fj|Japani|fo|Japan|frp|Japon|frr|Jaapan|fr|Japon|frnews|Catégorie:Japon|frquote|Japon|frvoyage|Japon|fur|Gjapon|fy|Japan|gag|Yaponiya|gan|日本|ga|An ᥓᥣᥱ ᥙᥣᥢᥱ|tet|Japaun|te|జపాన్|tg|Ҷопон|th|ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|thnews|หมวดหมู่:ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|tig|ጃፓን|ti|ጃፓን|tk|Ýaponiýa|tl|Hapon|tly|Japonijə|tn|Japan|tok|ma Nijon|to|Siapani|tpi|Siapan|trv|Tanah Tunux|tr|Japonya|trnews|Kategori:Japonya|trquote|Japonya|trvoyage|Japonya|ts|Japani|tt|Япония|tum|Japan|tw|Japan|tyv|Япония|ty|Tāpōnē|udm|Япония|ug|ياپونىيە|uk|Японія|uknews|Категорія:Японія|ukquote|Японія|ukvoyage|Японія|ur|جاپان|uz|Yaponiya|vec|Japon|vep|Japonii|ve|Japan|vi|Nhật Bản|vibooks|Các quốc gia/Nhật Bản|vivoyage|Nhật Bản|vls|Japan|vo|Yapän|war|Hapon|wa|Djapon|wo|Sapoŋ|wuu|日本|xal|Ниxуудин Нутг|xh|IJapani|xmf|იაპონია|yi|יאפאן|yo|Japan|za|Nyiedbonj|zea|Japan|zgh|ⵏⵉⴱⵓⵏ|zh_classical|日本|zh_min_nan|Ji̍t-pún|zh_yue|日本|zh|日本|zhnews|Category:日本|zhquote|日本|zhvoyage|日本|zu|IJaphani|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 4180005001154 == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Takamuno Shrine|lt=Takamuno Shrine|qid=Q135039552|1=Takamuno Shrine (Q135039552)}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '愛知県名古屋市昭和区御器所四丁目4番25号', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '愛知県名古屋市千種区今池', 'language': 'ja'} == Freebase ID (P646) == * /m/0jt44sf == topic's main category (P910) == * {{ill|Category:Gokiso Hachiman-gū|lt=Category:Gokiso Hachiman-gū|qid=Q51707353|1=Category:Gokiso Hachiman-gū}} [[EN:Gokiso Hachimangū]] [[FR:Gokiso Hachiman-gū]] [[JA:御器所八幡宮]] {{wikidata link|Q3109957}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Gokiso Hachimangū |画像 = [[File:Gokiso-Hachimangu02.jpg|250px]]
Torii gate and worship hall |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|qid=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|qid=Q11751|1=Nagoya}}{{ill|lt=Shōwa-ku|Q1155273|qid=Q1155273|1=Shōwa-ku}} Gokiso 4-chōme 4-25 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|8|44.30|N|136|55|24.28|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|qid=Q317997|lt=Homudawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Five Males and Three Females|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qid=Q2564508}}
{{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|Q8191796|qid=Q8191796|lt=Mizuhanome-no-Mikoto|1=Mizuhanome}}
{{ill|lt=Kikurihime|Q11619891|qid=Q11619891|lt=Kikurihime-no-Mikoto|1=Kikurihime}} |社格 = Former {{ill|lt=district shrine|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Unknown (one theory: {{ill|lt=9th century|Q8083|qid=Q8083|1=9th century}}) |本殿 = |別名 = Hassho Daimyōjin
Hassho Myōjin
Hachiman |例祭 = July 31 - August 1 (Akamaru Shinji)
October 14-15 (Grand Festival) |神事 = | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Gokiso
Hachimangū|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{Location map|Nagoya|label=Gokiso
Hachimangū|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Gokiso Hachimangū''' (御器所八幡宮) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} ({{ill|lt=Hachimangū|Q11390939|qid=Q11390939|1=Hachiman shrine}}) located in {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|qid=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}} {{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|qid=Q11751|1=Nagoya}} {{ill|lt=Shōwa-ku|Q1155273|qid=Q1155273|1=Shōwa-ku}} {{ill|lt=Gokiso|Q50984787|qid=Q50984787|1=Gokiso}} 4-chōme. The "Mononobe Shrine" of {{ill|lt=Aichi District|Q552687|qid=Q552687|lt=Aichi District|1=Aichi District}} in {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|qid=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, as listed in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'', has been identified with {{ill|lt=Mononobe Shrine|Q18235752|qid=Q18235752|lt=Mononobe Shrine|1=Mononobe Shrine (Higashi-ku, Nagoya)}} in {{ill|lt=Higashi-ku, Nagoya|Q578824|qid=Q578824|lt=Higashi-ku|1=Higashi-ku, Nagoya}} {{ill|lt=Tsutsui, Nagoya|Q62588744|qid=Q62588744|lt=Tsutsui|1=Tsutsui}}, but there is a theory that the listing originally referred to this shrine. === History === A {{ill|lt=ridgepole tag|Q8008834|qid=Q8008834|1=Munafuda}} from the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Muromachi period|1=UNKNOWN}} survives, indicating that the shrine was called '''Hassho Daimyōjin''' during the {{ill|lt=Middle Ages|Q12554|qid=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}}. There is a tradition that the shrine was established as an imperially-designated shrine by {{ill|lt=Emperor Ninmyō|Q349271|qid=Q349271|1=Emperor Ninmyō}} to guard the {{ill|lt=kimon|Q11673539|qid=Q11673539|1=Kimon}} (demon gate) of {{ill|lt=Atsuta Shrine|Q482065|qid=Q482065|1=Atsuta Shrine}}. The date of founding is unknown, but {{ill|lt=Shingūji|Q11589474|qid=Q11589474|lt=Iōzan Shingūji|1=Jingū-ji Temple (Nagoya)}}, which served as the shrine's {{ill|lt=jingūji|Q1074971|qid=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}} (shrine-temple) until the {{ill|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|qid=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}} in the Meiji era, is said to have been founded in {{ill|lt=850|Q30364|qid=Q30364|1=850}} ({{ill|lt=Kashō|Q1373157|qid=Q1373157|1=Kashō}} 3), suggesting that this shrine may have been founded before the {{ill|lt=9th century|Q8083|qid=Q8083|1=9th century}}. The shrine's {{ill|lt=inner sanctuary|Q11445659|qid=Q11445659|1=Okumiya}} was formerly located at the summit of {{ill|lt=Hachimanyama Kofun, Nagoya|Q17218723|qid=Q17218723|lt=Hachimanyama Kofun|1=Hachimanyama Kofun (Nagoya)}} to the northwest, where various {{ill|lt=Shinto rituals|Q11588671|qid=Q11588671|1=Shinto ritual}} are said to have been performed. There are a total of eight {{ill|lt=Buddhist statues|Q1000809|qid=Q1000809|1=Buddha in art}} estimated to date from the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kamakura period|1=UNKNOWN}}, and one theory holds that the name "Hassho Daimyōjin" derives from having eight {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=honji butsu|1=UNKNOWN}} (original Buddhist counterparts), meaning "Eight-deity Great Bright God." The oldest ridgepole tag dates from the renovation of Hassho Daimyōjin in {{ill|lt=1441|Q6402|qid=Q6402|1=1441}} ({{ill|lt=Kakitsu|Q1193238|qid=Q1193238|1=Kakitsu}} 1) by {{ill|lt=Sakuma clan|Q2975349|qid=Q2975349|lt=Sakuma Mimasaka-no-kami|1=Sakuma Mimasaka-no-kami}} Iekatsu and others. The Sakuma clan built Gokiso-nishi Castle ({{ill|lt=Biyō Shrine|Q11465530|qid=Q11465530|1=Biyō Shrine}}) and governed the surrounding area, and records of further construction and maintenance by the Sakuma clan survive from this period onward. It is also said that a ridgepole tag remains in which {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|qid=Q171977|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} recorded his gratitude for divine protection and his intention to make offerings immediately after the {{ill|lt=Battle of Sekigahara|Q234188|qid=Q234188|1=Battle of Sekigahara}}. In {{ill|lt=1659|Q6995|qid=Q6995|1=1659}} ({{ill|lt=Manji|Q1145295|qid=Q1145295|1=Manji (era)}} 2), the shrine was renamed Gokiso Hachimangū. During the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|qid=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, the shrine grounds included even more extensive temple precincts and a deep forest, which reportedly served as a landmark along the highway. After the Meiji era, the shrine was completely separated from its temple through {{ill|lt=haibutsu kishaku|Q702298|qid=Q702298|1=Haibutsu kishaku}} (anti-Buddhist movement), and today it is a shrine with precincts of over 1,300 tsubo (approximately 4,300 square meters). In ridgepole tags from before the {{ill|lt=Sengoku period|Q204023|qid=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}}, the {{ill|lt=negi|Q11594490|qid=Q11594490|1=Negi}} (chief priest) used the surname {{ill|lt=Fujiwara clan|Q641628|qid=Q641628|1=Fujiwara clan}}, but from the Edo period onward, the shrine's priests had been styled "Andō Yosandayū" for generations. Since the Andō family became extinct during the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|qid=Q3125096}}, the Hirao family currently serves as the {{ill|lt=gūji|Q11453500|qid=Q11453500|1=Gūji}} (head priest). The {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period|qid=Q184963}} local historian {{ill|lt=Tsuda Masao|Q22123474|qid=Q22123474|1=Tsuda Masanari}} advocated the theory that this shrine is the "Mononobe Shrine" of Aichi District recorded in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}''{{Cite web|url=https://jinja.nagoya/top/syouwaku/gokiso-hatimangu|title=御器所八幡宮|accessdate=2024-11-03|publisher=名古屋神社ガイド|author-オオタマサユキ|date=2019-03-14}}. The theory locating the {{ill|lt=Aichi District|Q552687|qid=Q552687|lt=Mononobe-gō|1=Aichi District}} of Aichi District in the Gokiso area is considered well-supported, based on the presence of multiple {{ill|lt=kofun|Q1141225|qid=Q1141225|1=Kofun}} (burial mounds) and {{ill|lt=abandoned temple sites|Q11486287|qid=Q11486287|1=former Buddhist temple}} in the Gokiso area, as well as the order of district entries in the ''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wamyō Ruijushō|1=UNKNOWN}}''{{Sfn|名古屋郷土文化会|1986|p=5}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Hachiman|Q261637|qid=Q261637|lt=Hachiman Ōkami|1=Hachiman}} ({{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|qid=Q317997|lt=Homudawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}}) * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Five Males and Three Females|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qid=Q2564508}} * {{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|Q8191796|qid=Q8191796|lt=Mizuhanome-no-Mikoto|1=Mizuhanome}} * {{ill|lt=Kikurihime|Q11619891|qid=Q11619891|lt=Kikurihime-no-Mikoto|1=Kikurihime}} * {{ill|lt=Kusanagi no Tsurugi|Q14944|qid=Q14944|lt=Yatsurugi Ōkami|1=Kusanagi no Tsurugi}} * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qid=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} * {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuyahime|Q1781862|qid=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuyahime-no-Mikoto|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} * {{ill|lt=Takakuraji|Q11668757|qid=Q11668757|lt=Takakura Ōkami|1=Takakuraji}} * {{ill|qid=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui|Q10935105|qid=Q10935105|lt=Sannō Ōkami|1=Oyamakui no Kami}} The deities up to Kikurihime-no-Mikoto are those recorded in the historical texts described below, while the deities from Yatsurugi Ōkami onward are those recorded in the shrine's own written history. === Subsidiary shrines === * Gokiso Tenmangū ** Originally a {{ill|lt=Tenjin shrine|Q11443279|qid=Q11443279|1=Tenjinsha}} that was located from {{ill|lt=Tenjin-chō, Nagoya|Q30934826|qid=Q30934826|lt=Tenjin-chō|1=Tenjin-chō}} to {{ill|lt=Kōbai-chō, Nagoya|Q26160583|qid=Q26160583|lt=Kōbai-chō|1=Kōbai-chō, Nagoya}} and was later merged into this shrine. The former site was reportedly called "Tenjin no Mori" (Tenjin Forest) or "Tenjinyama" (Tenjin Hill). It is said to enshrine {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|qid=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}, but Tsuda Masao has suggested that it may be an ancient shrine. * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Inari shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} * Hakuryū-sha === Access === === By car === * {{ill|lt=Nagoya City Road Hotta-Takaoka Line|Q11415129|qid=Q11415129|1=Nagoya Municipal Road Horita Takaoka Route}} Head east from the Shirogane 1-chōme intersection, approximately 600m (parking for 4 cars) === Public transportation === * {{ill|lt=Nagoya Municipal Subway Tsurumai Line|Q1043806|qid=Q1043806|1=Tsurumai Line}} Approximately 700m southwest from the Arahata intersection from {{ill|lt=Arahata Station|Q862961|qid=Q862961|1=Arahata Station}} * {{ill|lt=Nagoya City Bus|Q11414982|qid=Q11414982|1=Nagoya Municipal Bus}} Approximately 600m east from the "Tōkō-dōri 3-chōme" bus stop (Kanayama Route 26, Shōwa Circulation Route) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * Shōwa-ku 50th Anniversary Commemorative Project Committee, ed., ''History of Shōwa Ward'', {{ill|lt=1987|Q2429|qid=Q2429|1=1987}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=名古屋郷土文化会|title=郷土文化|volume=第40巻第3号（通巻145号）|publisher=名古屋郷土文化会|year=1986|date=1986-03-01|language=ja|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/ja/pid/6045176/1/5}} === External links === * [https://www.gokiso-jinja.com/ Gokiso Hachimangū] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Gokiso Hachimangū]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Gokiso Hachimangū]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagoya]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shōwa-ku, Nagoya]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Showa-ku, Nagoya]] [[Category:御器所]]
